elements.sent-2411-041: We consider the motion of $N$ run-and-tumble particles (RTPs) in one dimension, mutually interacting via a long-range repulsive inverse-square power-law potential of strength $g~(>0)$.
elements.sent-2411-042: In addition, the particles are subjected to a global confining harmonic potential with stiffness parameter $\lambda~(>0)$.

elements.sent-2411-261: In the steady state, we first focused on the density profile of the particles.
elements.sent-2411-262: As system activity increases, we observe an interesting change in the density profile: it transitions from a sharply peaked form to a smooth, bell-shaped distribution, with the Wigner semi-circle profile appearing in between.
elements.sent-2411-263: The first crossover connects a crystalline regime, marked by sharp peaks in the density profile, to a liquid-like phase with a smooth Wigner semi-circular profile devoid of such peaks.
elements.sent-2411-264: We have characterized quantitatively this crossover by computing the Lindemann's ratio, which compares the fluctuations in particle positions to the average gaps between successive particles.
elements.sent-2411-265: As observed in the active DBM~\cite{Touzo_EPL_2023 , we also found that the density profile in the steady state retains the shape of a Wigner semi-circle even at large activity.}
elements.sent-2411-266: However, at extremely high activity ($v_0\sim\mathcal{O (N)$), the density profile becomes bell-shaped.}
elements.sent-2411-267: To characterize this second crossover, we measured the deviation of the density profile from the Wigner semi-circle by computing the distance measure, $\chi$.
elements.sent-2411-268: Based on these values, we built a `phase' diagram in the $(a,1/\gamma)$ plane where $v_0=a N$ (see Fig.~\ref{fig:phase_diagram_together ).}
elements.sent-2411-269: Using a Hessian approximation, we computed the covariances of particle positions in the small activity (small $v_0$) limit, which also allowed us to obtain the variance of inter-particle gaps.
elements.sent-2411-270: Closed-form expressions for the variance of particle positions and inter-particle gaps were obtained both in the long persistence time limit ($\gamma \to 0$), where they can be expressed in terms of simple functions, and for finite $\gamma$, for large system sizes.
elements.sent-2411-271: These theoretical predictions remain valid at sufficiently large $v_0$ (in fact up to $v_0\sim \mathcal{O (N)$) in both regimes of $\gamma$.}
elements.sent-2411-272: By comparing the fluctuations of the particle positions with the other relevant length scales in the model, we also identified the crossover regions from weakly active to intermediate active, and finally to strongly active regimes.

elements.sent-2411-274: All these observations should be compared with what was found for the active DBM, which was studied in Refs.\cite{Touzo_EPL_2023, touzo_arxiv_2023 .}
elements.sent-2411-275: Qualitatively, the behavior of the two models is very similar.
elements.sent-2411-276: In particular, both models have the same `phase' diagram, with a first crossover from a crystal to a liquid regime and a second crossover to a bell-shaped density.
elements.sent-2411-277: Moreover, these crossovers occur for similar values of $v_0$ (note that the Refs.~\cite{Touzo_EPL_2023, touzo_arxiv_2023 use a different scaling of the parameters).}
elements.sent-2411-278: The differences between the two models become visible when looking more precisely at the finite $N$ effects in the density and at the fluctuations in the different regimes, in particular at the edges of the support.
elements.sent-2411-279: This study raises several interesting open questions, which call for further extensions.
elements.sent-2411-280: First, it is now evident that, for both the active DBM and the active CM, the density profile maintains its passive structure, i.e., the Wigner semi-circle, even at sufficiently large activity.
elements.sent-2411-281: It is thus natural to wonder whether a similar behavior occurs in other many-particle interacting systems, such as the harmonically confined Riesz gas~\cite{Agarwal_PRL_2019 .}
elements.sent-2411-282: A recent work addressed the properties of the Riesz gas on the circle \cite{Riesz2024 , but does not allow to study the effects related to the presence of an edge in the density.}
elements.sent-2411-283: Similarly, one can ask what happens if one shortens the range of the interactions~\cite{Kumar_PRE_2020, Santra_JPA_2024 .}
elements.sent-2411-284: Another challenging open question would be the description of the bell-shaped density profile, as well as the ubiquitous $1/x^3$ tail, emerging in the large activity limit in both the active DBM and active CM models.
